tasks.title: Set up Neon Postgres project with initial schema and RLS
tasks.description: Create Neon serverless Postgres database, implement row-level security policies, and seed initial users table structure for Auth.js adapter.
tasks.details: Use Neon console to create project (free tier sufficient for MVP). Enable Postgres RLS with policies: `CREATE POLICY user_isolation ON users FOR ALL USING (auth.uid() = id);`. Install @neondatabase/serverless driver v0.9.4 (latest 2026). Run initial migration with Drizzle ORM v0.33.0: `npx drizzle-kit generate:pg --schema ./src/db/schema.ts`. Seed default routines post-auth. Tech: Neon Postgres, Drizzle ORM 0.33.0, @neondatabase/serverless 0.9.4.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify RLS blocks cross-user access via test user accounts. Confirm schema with `drizzle-kit studio`. Check seeded data queryable only by owner.

subtasks.title: Create Neon Postgres project in free tier
subtasks.description: Sign up or log into Neon console and create a new serverless Postgres project using the free tier suitable for MVP development.
subtasks.details: Follow Neon console steps: enter project name, select Postgres version, database name, closest region, and click 'Create project'. Copy the connection string for later use.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify project appears in Neon dashboard with active status and free tier limits visible.

subtasks.title: Define initial users schema for Auth.js adapter
subtasks.description: Create the Drizzle ORM schema file defining the users table structure compatible with Auth.js adapter requirements.
subtasks.details: In ./src/db/schema.ts, define users table with essential columns like id (uuid primary key), email, name, etc. Ensure auth.uid() compatible id field for RLS policy.
subtasks.testStrategy: Validate schema syntax with TypeScript compiler and review structure matches Auth.js adapter docs.

subtasks.title: Install Neon driver and generate initial migration
subtasks.description: Install specified @neondatabase/serverless driver and Drizzle ORM, then generate and apply the Postgres migration.
subtasks.details: Run 'npm i @neondatabase/serverless@0.9.4 drizzle-orm@0.33.0 drizzle-kit'. Execute 'npx drizzle-kit generate:pg --schema ./src/db/schema.ts' and apply migration to Neon DB.
subtasks.testStrategy: Confirm migration files generated, run 'drizzle-kit studio' to verify users table exists in Neon database.

subtasks.title: Enable RLS and create user isolation policy
subtasks.description: Connect to Neon database and enable row-level security on users table with the specified isolation policy.
subtasks.details: Execute SQL: 'ALTER TABLE users ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY; CREATE POLICY user_isolation ON users FOR ALL USING (auth.uid() = id);'. Verify policy creation.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test policy blocks unauthorized access: insert test rows, query as different auth.uid() - expect no cross-user data visibility.

subtasks.title: Seed initial users table data and routines
subtasks.description: Implement and run seeding script for default users table structure post-auth setup, ensuring data respects RLS.
subtasks.details: Create seed script using Drizzle to insert initial data or routines. Run after auth integration. Ensure seeded data queryable only by respective owners.
subtasks.testStrategy: Execute seed script, verify data present via owner queries, confirm RLS blocks access for other users.

tasks.title: Implement day_plans database schema and migration
tasks.description: Create day_plans table matching REQ-001 TypeScript interface with proper indexes and constraints.
tasks.details: Drizzle schema: `dayPlans: pgTable('day_plans', { id: uuid().defaultRandom().primaryKey(), userId: uuid().notNull().references(() => users.id, { onDelete: 'cascade' }), date: date().notNull(), energy: 'low'|'medium'|'high'.notNull().default('medium'), workPriorities: jsonb().notNull().default('["","",""]'), priorityDone: jsonb().notNull().default('[false,false,false]'), nightMode: 'mic'|'light'|'rest'|'open'.notNull().default('open'), notToday: text(), morningRoutineId: uuid(), eveningRoutineId: uuid(), displaySentAt: timestamp(), createdAt: timestamp().defaultNow(), updatedAt: timestamp().defaultNow().$onUpdate(() => now()), }, (table) => ({ userDateUnique: unique().on(table.userId, table.date), dateIdx: index('day_plans_date_idx').on(table.date), }))`. Add unique constraint (user_id, date) for idempotency.
tasks.testStrategy: Insert duplicate (user,date) records fail. Query confirms indexes created. Migration runs clean on test DB branch.

subtasks.title: Verify and update Drizzle schema for day_plans table
subtasks.description: Review existing schema against REQ-001 interface and add any missing fields or constraints before migration.
subtasks.details: Ensure all fields match TypeScript interface: id, userId (FK cascade), date, energy enum, workPriorities jsonb, priorityDone jsonb, nightMode enum, notToday text, morningRoutineId uuid, eveningRoutineId uuid, displaySentAt timestamp. Confirm references to users table exist.
subtasks.testStrategy: Schema validation: run drizzle-kit generate and check generated SQL matches expected structure.

subtasks.title: Add unique constraint on (userId, date) for idempotency
subtasks.description: Implement unique index on userId and date columns to prevent duplicate day plans per user per date.
subtasks.details: Update table definition: unique().on(table.userId, table.date) named 'userDateUnique'. Verify existing index('day_plans_date_idx') is preserved. Ensure constraint name is explicit for migration clarity.
subtasks.testStrategy: Insert duplicate (userId, date) record → expect unique violation error.

subtasks.title: Add and validate indexes for query performance
subtasks.description: Ensure proper indexes on frequently queried fields: date and userId for efficient lookups.
subtasks.details: Confirm dateIdx: index('day_plans_date_idx').on(table.date). Consider composite index on (userId, date) if not covered by unique constraint. Add userId index if missing for FK lookups.
subtasks.testStrategy: Query EXPLAIN ANALYZE on SELECT by date and userId → confirm index usage.

subtasks.title: Generate and review database migration files
subtasks.description: Use Drizzle Kit to generate migration SQL from updated schema and validate generated migration.
subtasks.details: Run `drizzle-kit generate:pg` → review SQL for CREATE TABLE day_plans, indexes, constraints, defaults. Check foreign key references to users table. Ensure no data loss or conflicts with existing schema.
subtasks.testStrategy: Migration preview: dry-run on staging DB → no errors, correct table structure.

subtasks.title: Test migration execution on test database
subtasks.description: Execute migration on isolated test database branch and validate table structure post-migration.
subtasks.details: Create fresh test DB → run migration → verify: table exists, columns match schema, indexes/constraints present, defaults work, FK cascade configured. Test idempotency by attempting duplicate insert.
subtasks.testStrategy: Post-migration: DESCRIBE day_plans, test unique constraint violation, verify indexes with pg_indexes view.

tasks.title: Create routines and routine_steps database schemas
tasks.description: Implement REQ-003 schemas for reusable morning/evening routine checklists with steps.
tasks.details: Schemas: `routines: pgTable('routines', { id: uuid().defaultRandom().primaryKey(), userId: uuid().notNull().references(() => users.id), kind: 'morning'|'evening'.notNull(), name: varchar(100).notNull(), }), routineSteps: pgTable('routine_steps', { id: uuid().defaultRandom().primaryKey(), routineId: uuid().notNull().references(() => routines.id, { onDelete: 'cascade' }), label: text().notNull(), durationMinutes: integer(), trigger: text(), minimumVersion: text(), position: integer().notNull().default(0), }, (table) => ({ routinePosUnique: unique().on(table.routineId, table.position), }))`. Seed 1 morning + 1 evening routine per user on signup.
tasks.testStrategy: Confirm cascade delete works. Verify position uniqueness per routine. Seed script creates expected defaults.

subtasks.title: Define routines and routine_steps schemas in Drizzle
subtasks.description: Create the PostgreSQL table definitions for routines and routine_steps tables using the provided schema specifications in the database schema file.
subtasks.details: Use pgTable from Drizzle ORM. Implement routines table with id (uuid PK), userId (references users.id), kind ('morning'|'evening'), name (varchar(100)). Implement routine_steps with id (uuid PK), routineId (references routines.id with cascade delete), label (text), durationMinutes (integer), trigger (text), minimumVersion (text), position (integer default 0). Add unique constraint on (routineId, position).
subtasks.testStrategy: Run drizzle-kit studio to verify schema structure matches specifications including foreign keys and unique constraints.

subtasks.title: Generate and apply database migration
subtasks.description: Use Drizzle Kit to generate PostgreSQL migration from schema changes and apply it to the Neon database.
subtasks.details: Execute 'npx drizzle-kit generate:pg --schema ./src/db/schema.ts' then 'npx drizzle-kit push:pg'. Verify migration creates tables with correct constraints using Neon console or pgAdmin.
subtasks.testStrategy: Check Neon database shows new tables with correct columns, indexes, and foreign key relationships via SQL query or Drizzle Studio.

subtasks.title: Implement routine_steps position uniqueness constraint
subtasks.description: Ensure no duplicate positions within the same routine by properly configuring the unique index on routine_steps.
subtasks.details: In schema definition, use unique().on(table.routineId, table.position) as specified. Test by attempting to insert duplicate position for same routineId - should fail.
subtasks.testStrategy: Insert two steps with same routineId and position=1; second insert should throw unique constraint violation error.

subtasks.title: Create seed script for default routines
subtasks.description: Write database seed function to create 1 morning and 1 evening routine with steps for each new user on signup.
subtasks.details: Create seed script in /db/seed.ts using Drizzle transactions. For each user, insert default routines: morning routine with 3-5 steps (wake up, exercise, breakfast), evening routine with steps (dinner, reading, sleep prep). Use sequential positions starting from 1.
subtasks.testStrategy: Create test user, trigger signup seed, verify exactly 2 routines (morning+evening) with appropriate steps exist for that userId only.

subtasks.title: Test cascade delete and RLS integration
subtasks.description: Verify routine_steps cascade deletion works and tables respect existing RLS user isolation policies.
subtasks.details: Test deleting a routine cascades to delete all its steps. Confirm user A cannot access user B's routines due to RLS policy on users table extending to routines via foreign key.
subtasks.testStrategy: 1) Delete routine, confirm steps gone. 2) As user A, query user B's routines - should return empty. 3) Verify seed creates user-specific routines visible only to owner.

tasks.title: Set up Next.js 15 App Router project with Tailwind and shadcn/ui

Tech: Next.js 15.0.0, Tailwind CSS 4.0, shadcn/ui 0.9.0.

subtasks.title: Set up ESLint, Prettier, and environment variable

tasks.title: Implement Auth.js magic-link authentication with Neon adapter

Custom Neon adapter: `new DrizzleNeonAdapter({ connection: { url: process.env.NEON_URL! } })`. Add middleware protection for /app/* routes. Magic link template via Resend. Sessions use secure cookies (httpOnly, secure, sameSite: 'lax').
tasks.testStrategy: Send magic link → click → authenticated session. Verify users table populated. Test logout + token expiry.

subtasks.title: Implement Custom Magic Link Email Template via Resend

subtasks.testStrategy: Send magic link → click → authenticated session with secure cookies. Verify users table populated. Test logout + token expiry after maxAge.

tasks.title: Implement DayPlan CRUD API endpoints (REQ-001)
tasks.description: Create POST/GET/PATCH endpoints for DayPlan with idempotency, timezone handling, and validation.
tasks.details: Route handlers `/api/v1/day-plans/route.ts`, `/api/v1/day-plans/today/route.ts`, `/api/v1/day-plans/[id]/route.ts`. Use server actions for client calls. Idempotency: `SELECT * FROM day_plans WHERE user_id = $1 AND date = $2`. Timezone: `date.toLocaleDateString('en-CA', { timeZone: user.timezone })`. Zod validation: `z.object({ energy: z.enum(['low','medium','high']), work_priorities: z.array(z.string()).length(3), ... })`. Update `display_sent_at` on send.
tasks.testStrategy: POST idempotent, GET today returns local date, PATCH partial updates work. Integration tests hit test DB.

subtasks.details: Use SQL: SELECT * FROM day_plans WHERE user_id = $1 AND date = $2 for idempotency check. Apply Zod schema: z.object({ energy: z.enum(['low','medium','high']), work_priorities: z.array(z.string()).length(3), ... }). Convert date using date.toLocaleDateString('en-CA', { timeZone: user.timezone }).
subtasks.testStrategy: Test idempotent POST returns existing record on duplicate; invalid Zod data returns 400; valid create inserts new record.

subtasks.testStrategy: GET today returns correct DayPlan for user's local date; different timezones return expected records; 404 for no plan today.

subtasks.details: In /api/v1/day-plans/[id]/route.ts, fetch DayPlan by id and user_id ownership check, use Zod for partial schema validation, apply updates with Drizzle, support fields like priorityDone, notToday.
subtasks.testStrategy: PATCH partial updates modify only provided fields; invalid id or non-owned returns 404/403; Zod validation rejects bad data.

subtasks.testStrategy: Client calls server actions trigger correct endpoint logic; auth required; errors propagate to client.

tasks.details: Route handler with `Authorization: Bearer <device_token>`. Verify token in `device_tokens` table (scope: 'display:read'). Return DisplayPayload + ETag. Support `If-None-Match` → 304. p95 <200ms via connection pooling. Graceful 503 on DB failure.
tasks.testStrategy: curl with valid token → 200 payload. Invalid token → 401. ETag match → 304. Load test 1000 reqs <200ms p95.

subtasks.testStrategy: curl -H 'Authorization: Bearer valid_token' → route hit logged. Missing header → 401 early.

subtasks.title: Implement device token verification middleware
subtasks.description: Verify device_token exists in device_tokens table with scope 'display:read' using connection pooling.
subtasks.details: Create middleware verifyDeviceToken(req, res, next): Query `SELECT device_id FROM device_tokens WHERE token = $1 AND scope @> 'display:read' AND expires_at > NOW()`. req.deviceId = result.device_id. Use pg-pool for p95<200ms.
subtasks.testStrategy: Valid token → middleware passes with req.deviceId. Invalid/expired/missing scope → 401 Unauthorized.

subtasks.title: Fetch and cache today's DisplayPayload with ETag
subtasks.description: Query today's display data scoped to verified device, generate ETag from content hash, support DB connection pooling.
subtasks.details: In handler: const today = new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0]; Query day_plans JOIN users ON device_id for date=today. Compute etag = crypto.createHash('md5').update(JSON.stringify(payload)).digest('hex'). Return { ...DisplayPayload, etag }.
subtasks.testStrategy: Fresh data → 200 with ETag in response. Verify payload matches day_plans for device.

subtasks.title: Add ETag If-None-Match 304 support
subtasks.description: Compare client If-None-Match header with generated ETag, return 304 Not Modified if match.
subtasks.details: Before sending response: if (req.headers['if-none-match'] === etag) { return res.status(304).end(); }. Set res.set('ETag', etag) on 200 responses. Handle weak etags with W/ prefix if needed.
subtasks.testStrategy: curl -H 'If-None-Match: exact_etag' → 304. Mismatch → 200 new ETag. No header → 200 always.

tasks.details: Arduino-ESP32 v3.0.0. WiFiAP + DNSServer for captive portal. Web form collects SSID/password + device token. Store NVS: `preferences.putString('ssid', ssid); preferences.putString('token', token);`. Reference: Waveshare 4.2" e-ink.

subtasks.details: Use WiFi.mode(WIFI_AP_STA); WiFi.scanNetworks() to cache up to 8 SSIDs. Switch to WIFI_AP, WiFi.softAP('ESP32-Setup'), dnsServer.start(53, '*', WiFi.softAPIP()). Handle captive probes like /generate_204.[1]

subtasks.title: Handle form submission and store credentials in NVS

subtasks.details: server.on('/connect', HTTP_POST, handleConnect): preferences.begin('prov', true); preferences.putString('ssid', ssid); putString('pass', pass); putString('token', token); preferences.end(); Set flag shouldConnectToWiFi=true.[1]
subtasks.testStrategy: Submit form, check serial logs confirm storage, verify preferences.getString() retrieves values.

subtasks.title: Implement boot sequence and station mode connection logic
subtasks.description: In setup(), check NVS for credentials; if present connect in STA mode, else start AP provisioning.
subtasks.details: connectToStoredWiFi(): Load from NVS, WiFi.mode(WIFI_STA); WiFi.begin(ssid, pass); timeout 10s, fail→restart AP. setup(): connectToStoredWiFi() then setupAP() if not connected.[1]

tasks.title: ESP32 display endpoint polling and JSON parsing
tasks.description: 15-minute polling cycle with HTTPClient + ArduinoJson (REQ-008).
tasks.details: ArduinoJson 7.0.3 + HTTPClient. `if (millis() - lastPoll > 15*60*1000) { http.GET("/api/v1/display/today", token); parse payload; }`. Handle 304, errors, not_planned. Configurable interval via NVS.
tasks.testStrategy: Mock server → polls correctly → parses JSON → handles errors gracefully.

subtasks.title: Implement NVS storage for configurable polling interval
subtasks.description: Create non-volatile storage to read and write the polling interval in minutes, defaulting to 15 minutes.
subtasks.details: Use ESP32 NVS library to create a namespace 'display' with key 'poll_interval_min'. Read on boot, validate range 5-60 min, fallback to 15. Write function for future config changes via WiFi or button.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify read/write persistence after reboot. Check invalid values fallback to default. Serial log values.

subtasks.title: Add HTTP polling timer logic with configurable interval
subtasks.description: Implement millis()-based timer that triggers every poll_interval_ms, tracking lastPoll time.
subtasks.details: Declare unsigned long lastPoll = 0; In loop(): if (millis() - lastPoll > poll_interval_ms) { triggerPoll(); lastPoll = millis(); }. Handle millis() overflow. Use uint32_t for interval from NVS.
subtasks.testStrategy: Manual timer test: advance millis(), verify trigger exactly at interval. Check overflow safety.

subtasks.title: Implement HTTP GET request with device token and ETag support
subtasks.description: Send authenticated GET to /api/v1/display/today with Bearer token, handle 304 Not Modified response.
subtasks.details: Use HTTPClient: setUrl(), addHeader('Authorization', 'Bearer ' + token), addHeader('If-None-Match', currentETag). Check httpCode: 200→parse, 304→skip, others→error. Store new ETag from 'ETag' header.
subtasks.testStrategy: Mock server: valid token→200, invalid→401, ETag match→304. Verify ETag storage.

subtasks.title: Parse DisplayPayload JSON with ArduinoJson 7.0.3
subtasks.description: Deserialize HTTP response body into structured DisplayPayload, handle 'not_planned' state.
subtasks.details: Use ArduinoJson 7: DeserializationError err = deserializeJson(doc, payload); if(err) error. Extract fields: doc['state'], doc['date_label'], doc['priorities'], doc['routine']. Handle missing fields gracefully.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test with mock JSON strings: valid payload, not_planned, malformed→error logged.

subtasks.title: Add comprehensive error handling and logging
subtasks.description: Handle WiFi disconnect, HTTP errors, JSON parse failures, and log status for debugging.
subtasks.details: In triggerPoll(): WiFi.status()!=WL_CONNECTED→log/retry. httpCode!=200&&304→log code. Parse fail→log 'JSON parse error'. Success→log 'Poll OK, state: X'. Retry logic: 3 attempts max.
subtasks.testStrategy: Simulate failures: disconnect WiFi→retry log, bad JSON→parse error, 500→HTTP error logged.

tasks.title: ESP32 deep sleep and battery optimization
tasks.description: ≥14 days battery life on 2000mAh with 15-min polls (REQ-008).
tasks.details: ESP32 deep sleep: `esp_deep_sleep_start()` after poll/render. Wake every 15min: RTC alarm. Current: ~10uA sleep, 100mA poll/render → 14+ days. NVS fallback to last-good payload if >24h offline.
tasks.testStrategy: Battery drain test: verify <10uA sleep current. Survives 24h offline → renders last payload.

subtasks.title: Implement 15-minute RTC deep sleep wake-up timer
subtasks.description: Configure ESP32 RTC alarm to wake device precisely every 15 minutes (900 seconds) for polling cycle.
subtasks.details: Use `esp_sleep_enable_timer_wakeup(15 * 60 * 1000000LL);` before `esp_deep_sleep_start()`. Ensure timer accuracy ±5% over 24h period. Handle wake cause verification with `esp_sleep_get_wakeup_cause()`.
subtasks.testStrategy: Log wake timestamps over 24h, verify interval 15min ±10s. Test timer drift after 100 cycles.

subtasks.title: Add NVS storage for last-good payload and offline timer
subtasks.description: Store parsed JSON payload in NVS with 24h offline timestamp. Fallback to last-good on startup if offline >24h.
subtasks.details: Use `nvs_set_str('last_payload', json_str); nvs_set_i32('last_poll', now());`. On boot: `if (now() - last_poll > 24*3600) render(last_payload);`. Partition: 'storage', key size limit 4000 bytes.
subtasks.testStrategy: Mock clock advance 25h, verify renders last payload. Clear NVS → fresh poll. Validate JSON parse after NVS read.

subtasks.title: Optimize poll/render cycle to <3 seconds active time
subtasks.description: Minimize WiFi connect + HTTP poll + JSON parse + display render to under 3s total before deep sleep.
subtasks.details: Static IP, `WiFi.mode(WIFI_STA); WiFi.config(ip, gw, mask);`. HTTP timeout 2s. ArduinoJson minimal parse. Skip display if payload unchanged (CRC check). Target: 100mA × 3s = 0.083mAh/cycle.
subtasks.testStrategy: Time each phase with `millis()`. Verify total <3000ms over 50 cycles. Monitor current with INA219 during active phase.

subtasks.title: Verify and optimize deep sleep current to ≤10μA
subtasks.description: Achieve measured deep sleep current of 10μA or less by disabling peripherals and optimizing power circuit.
subtasks.details: GPIO0/2 pull-down, `esp_sleep_pd_config(ESP_PD_DOMAIN_RTC_PERIPH, ESP_PD_OPTION_OFF);`. Disable WiFi/BT before sleep: `WiFi.disconnect(true); esp_bluedroid_disable();`. Remove LEDs, use low-IQ regulator.
subtasks.testStrategy: Multimeter serial measurement: confirm ≤10μA stable for 10min. Test across 3.0-4.2V battery range. Compare before/after optimizations.

subtasks.title: Validate 14+ day battery life and integrate full cycle
subtasks.description: Combine all components: poll→parse→render→NVS→sleep. Verify 2000mAh lasts ≥14 days (≤3.57mA average).
subtasks.details: Full cycle: wake→poll Task23 endpoint→parse→NVS update→render display→sleep 15min. Calculate: (100mA×3s + 10μA×897s)/900s ≈0.334mA avg → 2000mAh/0.334mA=5980h=249days theoretical.
subtasks.testStrategy: Accelerated test: 100 cycles (25h), measure total mAh drain, extrapolate to 14 days. Real 48h test confirms <0.4mA avg. Offline 24h→renders last payload.
